Skill: 2H Blunt Atk Delay: 36
DMG: 24 Dmg Bonus: 132
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

Effect: Soul Bond (Combat, Casting Time: Instant) at Level 46
WT: 10.0 Size: LARGE
Class: SHD
Race: ALL

Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

▲ Page top
Proc Damage
QuoteReply
# Jul 29 2003 at 9:38 AM Rating: Excellent
Darknyss
Scholar
551 posts
The proc is a "Splurt" type lifetap, doing more damage the longer it's on.

When it procs, the mob and yourself are hit with "SoulBondEffect." It does 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, and then 30 damage to the mob and heals you respectively, on each tick, for a total of 105 hps. If it procs while you already have the SoulBondEffect, the damage sets back to 5 and then continues.

IMHO, Great weapon to learn 2HB, not only does it do decent damage for its delay, but also adds a great downtime reducer, as I've had it proc fairly often with only a little over 100 dex...

plus it looks cool =)
____________________________
Pain Lord Darknyss Grimscale
92 Iksar Crusader of the Greenmist

#REDACTED, Posted: Feb 12 2002 at 1:42 PM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) You bunch of Morons. I am a 34 warrio and i Double for more then 200. ( 50, 50, 72, 65) useing Gornaga spiked club and Fist of zek. got 190 str unbuffed. So, yeah dunno. Guess im just cool and your not. Meh. But PC on soul binder on my server is bout 3 4k? Oh Btw 2hs users. go to Maidens eye, get Strenght of Grodan forget exact stats, No drop, and with a 2hs skill of 30 i doubled 160, 170. # Aug 02 2004 at 5:55 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591809373045
5 posts
Just FYI, when you say you hit for (50, 50, 72, 65), that would be a quad, not a double. Double means 2, in case you didn't realize. What you're actually saying is that you can double for 137 or so. Get your facts straight before you try to call anybody else a moron.

QuoteReply
# Feb 01 2002 at 12:46 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592253154232
13 posts
I just looted one of these off the Skeletal Captain in KC last night. Not a bad weapon. Although I have the Strength of Grodan (41/51 STR 25 STA 10 wieght 1.5 2hs) and would rather use my Ebon Mace with Sarnak Battle Shield for the AC. The real benefit of any blunt weapon is they always have a chance to be counted as a bash therefore interrupting a caster. Anyone on The Rathe server in need of this weapon look me up.....selling for 2k
Vittorrio
53rd Reaver
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Feb 24 2003 at 9:37 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591786442997
4 posts
Casting has three factors that interrupt it: first and most obvious is movement. Everytime you are hit it moves your avatar a small amount unless you are backed into a corner which is why they say to back yourself in a corner as a caster in PvP (that and to get away from rogue Backstabs). The second and second most obvious is damage. The more damage dealt to you between the time you start casting the spell and the time you finish, the more likely you will be interrupted. The third is bashes, slams, and stuns, which not only interrupt your casting but stop you mid cast and interrupt your casting. This cannot even be mitigated by Runes. If you have a rune up and you are bashed (kicked slammed etc) there is still a chance it will interupt you and still a chance it will stun you, even if it didn't do damage to you. Sorry i had to clear that up.

This weapon is very kewl, by the way. Procs a 50 per tick over 5 ticks life leach at 45 and looks like a staff with a crescent moon on either end. (and the translucent stuff everyone is seeing is actually a bug, if looked at from the wrong angles the crescent moves lose part of there graphic).
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# May 12 2002 at 7:50 AM Rating: Default
__DEL__1592757930748
53 posts
no they dont (in regards to blunt weapons)
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Dec 08 2002 at 5:41 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591780199935
Scholar
63 posts
What he is talking about is not a stun, but a casting interupt. Blunt weapons have a slight knockback effect on them, as do many weapons, and that may cause interruption, just as casters trying to walk while casting does.
▲ Page top
RE: Hmm
QuoteReply
# Sep 08 2002 at 12:42 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1591781085237
61 posts
Um, yeah, get the facts straight first please. Blunt weapons DO NOT count as a bash. Bash, Slam, Kick, and Stun spells are all I know of that stun. And since you said blunt weapons have a chance to bash, that would mean that the Soul Binder weapon would also have a chance to bash. What I'm trying to say is you're an idiot.

#Anonymous, Posted: Nov 06 2001 at 6:36 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) 8,643pp
The effect specifics:
QuoteReply
# Jun 13 2001 at 6:54 AM Rating: Excellent
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
Soul Bond is a DOT lifetap that will not take hold if you have rabies or strong disease, etc.

One tick after the proc, you will gain 5 health. One tick after that, you gain 10 health... then 15, 20, 25, and finally 30.

If you proc again befor reaching the 30 dmg marker, it starts again at 5.

Total dmg: 105 at level 51. Don't know if the duration is level based, however.
▲ Page top
RE: The effect specifics:
QuoteReply
# Jun 17 2001 at 11:16 AM Rating: Default
Anonymous
View User Forum
Anonymous
I was once intrested in a Soul Binder, it has nice stats, a proc, and looks damn cool. Yet, a bargain came my way from an unknowing warrior. He needed a Wurmslayer, and had a very nice 2hs (which is what I use) The weapon he offered for Wurm had a great ratio and had a few +stats. It is called a Green Jade Halberd. It's stats are as follows: 28/38 +7 AGI +5 Resist disease and poison +50 Hps and has +STA and CHA as well. I found out it is worth anywhere from 7k-14k...a bit more than Wurm, heh. It is SK only too. Since I had gotten this great deal I am no longer intrested in a Soul Binder, and to all those who have money and use 2hs and don't want to switch to 2hb just to use a Soul Binder, try to get a deal on a Green Jade Halberd. I am level 25 DeSK and can double for 84 DMG!
~Nakrull Wraithraven
